_metadata.description: IRIS² (Infrastructure for Resilience, Interconnectivity and Security by Satellite) constellation orbital parameters compiled from Wikipedia and EU press releases.
_metadata.full_name: Infrastructure for Resilience, Interconnectivity and Security by Satellite
notes: Multi-orbit constellation (LEO + MEO) for secure government communications and broadband for citizens.
Cost: approximately €10.5 billion (€6.5 billion public funds).
Prime contractor shortlist: Airbus vs Aerospacelab (decision expected by end 2025).
Detailed orbital parameters (inclinations, number of planes) are NOT publicly specified as of early 2026.
IRIS² is the EU's sovereign connectivity constellation, independent of US/Chinese systems.
Launch vehicles: expected to use Ariane 6.

shells.notes: LEO layer; altitude reported as ~1,200 km. Inclination and plane configuration not yet publicly disclosed.
MEO layer at ~8,000 km. Provides coverage complementary to LEO layer. Detailed orbital parameters not public.

derived_orbital_parameters.description: Pre-computed orbital parameters for each shell

services.government: Secure sovereign communications for EU member states
services.commercial: Broadband internet for EU citizens, including underserved areas
